title: tephrosia marginata
intro: Tephrosia marginata, also known as the "Hawaiian cowhage" or "beach cowhage," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a climbing vine that is native to tropical regions, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as arthritis, fever, and skin irritations, and has been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Tephrosia marginata can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions.
